summary: Large decompilation pass for Sheik neutral special (`ftSk_SpecialN.c`), bringing the file to 98.89% matched over 3.87 KB. The PR replaced most placeholder address-range comments with C implementations for Sheik needle start/loop/cancel/end animation, IASA, physics, collision, and needle shooting/helper routines, while retaining the assembly object because the file was not yet 100% matching. It also updated function prototypes, Sheik type comments, held-needle item prototypes, a symbol name for `fn_80112D44` to `ftSk_Sheik_ShootNeedles`, and made `tools/split_suggester.py` tolerate missing external map-name data.
what_changed: Expanded `src/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialN.c` from mostly literals/address-range comments into C implementations for the Sheik needle neutral-special action states.
Implemented or added stubs for many action-state callbacks: ground/air enter, start/loop/cancel/end animation callbacks, IASA callbacks, physics callbacks, and collision callbacks.
Added C for helper routines including `ftSk_SpecialN_80111FBC`, `ftSk_SpecialN_801120D4`, and `ftSk_Sheik_ShootNeedles`.
Renamed the assembly/config symbol at `0x80112D44` from `fn_80112D44` to `ftSk_Sheik_ShootNeedles` in `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialN.s` and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.
Updated `ftSk_SpecialN.h` include guard from `GALE01_1120E8` to `GALE01_111FBC`, added `ft/forward.h`, and changed most prototypes from generic `HSD_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*`.
Added prototype coverage for `ftSk_SpecialN_80111FBC` and the needle-shoot callback address in `ftSk_SpecialN.h`.
Added held-needle item function prototypes in `itseakneedleheld.h`: `it_802AFD8C`, `it_802AFEA8`, and `it_802B19AC`.
Annotated Sheik structs/unions in `types.h` with observed Fighter offsets such as `fp->fv.sk`, `fp->dat_attrs`, and `fp->mv.sk`.
Removed local static forward declarations for `ftSk_SpecialN_80111FBC` and `ftSk_SpecialS_CheckAndDestroyChain` from `ftSk_Init.c`, relying on headers instead.
Changed `tools/split_suggester.py` so map-name parsing is wrapped in a try/except and falls back to an empty name map when external map lookup fails.
smart_moves: Kept the original assembly file/object in place because the C file was only 98.89% matched, avoiding an unsafe legacy build removal.
Converted prototypes to `Fighter_GObj*` where fighter callbacks are expected, which makes the decompiled code more type-specific than generic `HSD_GObj*` usage.
Preserved exact matching pressure with local temporaries, explicit callback assignments, address comments, decomp.me scratch links, and `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused-stack padding where needed.
Renamed an anonymous address-based symbol to the behavior-oriented `ftSk_Sheik_ShootNeedles`, improving searchability and callback readability.
Added item prototypes close to the held-needle item header instead of leaving calls implicit, supporting further item/fighter boundary decomp work.
Made split-suggester robust to missing or failing external map-name sources instead of failing the whole suggestion run.
decomp_lessons: Do not remove an `.s.o` from legacy object lists merely because a C file has a high match percentage; retain assembly until the C file is 100% matching or the build strategy explicitly supports partial replacement.
For fighter action-state decompilation, matching often requires modeling callback fields such as `take_dmg_cb`, `death2_cb`, and `accessory4_cb`, not just visible motion-state transitions.
Ground and air versions of a move can share structure but differ in physics/collision helpers and fall-special behavior; keep them separate when the original code does.
Offset comments on fighter vars, dat attributes, and motion vars are valuable scaffolding when field names are still unknown.
Small empty IASA callbacks are legitimate decomp targets and should be emitted as explicit empty functions when the original functions are 4-byte `blr`s.
When function-local stack shape differs, guarded unused stack arrays under `MUST_MATCH` can document and preserve a matching workaround without pretending the data is semantically used.
naming_conventions: Address-only symbols should be promoted when behavior is known: `fn_80112D44` became `ftSk_Sheik_ShootNeedles`.
Sheik fighter code uses the `ftSk_` prefix and action-state names like `SpecialNStart`, `SpecialNLoop`, `SpecialNCancel`, `SpecialNEnd`, with `Air` variants for aerial states.
Unknown helper functions can retain address-suffixed names such as `ftSk_SpecialN_80111FBC` and `ftSk_SpecialN_801120D4` until a behavior name is certain.
Headers in this area keep address comments before declarations, e.g. `/* 1120E8 */`, which helps map prototypes back to text addresses.
Struct offset comments such as `// x222C (fp->fv.sk._)` and `// x2340 (fp->mv.sk._._)` are useful conventions for partially understood fighter data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used decomp.me scratch references in comments for individual functions, indicating iterative function-level matching.
Retained literal float array `ftSk_SpecialN_803CC598` and used it in the needle-shooting helper, including a zero value accessed via the array rather than only a literal in one helper.
Used explicit motion-state IDs such as `0x155`, `0x156`, `0x159`, `0x15A`, `0x15B`, and `0x15C` and preserved original-style float arguments to `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`.
Used explicit comparisons and casts around Sheik needle counters such as `fv.sk.x222C` and `fv.sk.x2230` to match codegen.
Added guarded unused stack arrays under `MUST_MATCH` in functions with noted unused stack requirements.
Updated assembly labels and the global symbols file together for the `ftSk_Sheik_ShootNeedles` rename, keeping asm references and symbol metadata aligned.
review_feedback: A commenter asked whether `/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialN.s.o` should be removed from `obj_files.mk` for legacy support.
ribbanya replied that the C file was not 100% matching, so the asm file needed to remain for now.
No line-level review comments were present in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that bring `ftSk_SpecialN.c` from 98.89% to 100% and remove or retire the corresponding asm object.
Search for subsequent cleanup of `ftSk_ShootNeedles` versus `ftSk_Sheik_ShootNeedles` naming in headers and callbacks.
Look for later PRs that replace Sheik motion variable fields `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `x222C`, and `x2230` with semantic names.
Check whether `tools/split_suggester.py` received more robust map parsing changes after this try/except fallback.
Compare Zelda/Sheik special-N decomp patterns for shared needle or projectile callback handling.
